refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process can be decomposed into data acquisition (collecting telemetry and sensor data) and data processing (model update computation and dissemination). This decomposition isolates sources of delay and enables targeted design solutions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data acquisition start to updated model availability; separately measure data acquisition latency and processing latency to verify sub-FRs.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire telemetry and sensor data from vehicles and site within required latency.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within required latency after data acquisition.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry network with prioritized data streams.
DP2: Implement real-time data processing pipeline with parallel computation and incremental model updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address data acquisition and processing latency respectively.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing pipeline timing.
Dependency on network link performance affecting both DP1 and DP2.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interface boundaries (e.g., telemetry interface).

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Risk of telemetry data loss or delay impacting processing timeliness.
Processing algorithms must be optimized for real-time constraints.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and processing load; consider fallback or buffering strategies if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry acquisition, data processing, and worksite model update.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion.
Limited processing; mainly data source, so low containment for update latency.
May process some data locally but not primary for model update.
Focused on safety alerts, not on worksite model update.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency functionality within the NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and diffusion risks.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded.
Dependency on network link performance external to NGHE Solution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular subcomponents within NGHE Solution for scalability; monitor network link health.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into measurable sub-FRs; selected DPs logically address these sub-FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion by centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution module.
Network link dependency remains a risk but is a known interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as owner; interfaces are well-defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end and per sub-FR; module boundaries align with verification points.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of telemetry acquisition and processing to NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance and consider fallback buffering.
Plan for scalability within NGHE Solution to avoid bottlenecks.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and allocation meets the requirement with clear FR-DP-IM mapping, manageable coupling, and verifiable interfaces. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire telemetry and sensor data from vehicles and site within required latency.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within required latency after data acquisition.

revise.why: Current decomposition and allocation are sufficient and consistent with constraints.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Processing algorithms can meet real-time constraints.
open_risks: Potential processing bottlenecks under high load.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry acquisition and processing pipeline.
Network link performance statistics under operational conditions.
